name: elastic-kibana
label: Elastic Kibana
description: Elastic Kibana provides a powerful UI for interacting with the Elastic Stack. It enables users to search, visualize, and manage data from Elasticsearch, build dashboards, monitor systems, and secure their environment.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.description: Specify the URL of the Elastic server to connect and perform automated operations.
fields.name: url
fields.tooltip: Specify the URL of the Elastic server to connect and perform automated operations.

fields.title: API Key
fields.description: Specify the API Key to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations
fields.name: api_key
fields.tooltip: Specify the API Key to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.description: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true.
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true.

operations.title: Get All Data Views
operations.description: Retrieve a list of all data views from Elastic Kibana.

operations.title: Get Saved Queries
operations.description: Retrieve a list of all saved queries from Elastic Kibana.

operations.title: Create Live Query
operations.description: Create and run a live query in Elastic Kibana based on the input parameters that ypu have specified.

parameters.title: Query
parameters.description: Specify the SQL query to execute in Elastic Kibana. For e.g select * from uptime;
parameters.name: query
parameters.tooltip: Specify the SQL query to execute in Elastic Kibana. For e.g select * from uptime;

parameters.title: Agent IDs
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of agent IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: agent_ids
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of agent IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana.

parameters.title: Agent Platforms
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of platforms IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: agent_platforms
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of platforms IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana.

parameters.title: Agent Policy IDs
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of policy IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: agent_policy_ids
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of policy IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana. For e.g ["policy_id_1", "policy_id_2", ...]

parameters.title: Alert IDs
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of alert IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: alert_ids
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of alert IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana. For e.g ["alert_id_1", "alert_id_2", ...]

parameters.title: Case IDs
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of alert IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: case_ids
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of alert IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana. For e.g ["case_id_1", "case_id_2", ...]

parameters.title: ECS Mapping
parameters.description: Map osquery results columns or static values to Elastic Common Schema (ECS) fields.
parameters.name: ecs_mapping
parameters.tooltip: Map osquery results columns or static values to Elastic Common Schema (ECS) fields. For e.g {"host.uptime":{"field":"total_seconds"}}}

parameters.title: Event IDs
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of event IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: event_ids
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of event IDs to create and execute a live query in Elastic Kibana. For e.g ["event_id_1", "event_id_2", ...]

parameters.title: Metadata
parameters.description: Specify the custom metadata object associated with the live query.
parameters.name: metadata
parameters.tooltip: Specify the custom metadata object associated with the live query.

parameters.title: Pack ID
parameters.description: Specify the ID of the pack you want to run in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: pack_id
parameters.tooltip: Specify the ID of the pack you want to run in Elastic Kibana.

operations.title: Get Live Query Results
operations.description: Retrieve the results of a live query using the query action ID from Elastic Kibana.

parameters.title: Query ID
parameters.description: Specify the ID of the live query result you want to retrieve.
parameters.name: id
parameters.tooltip: Specify the ID of the live query result you want to retrieve.

parameters.title: Query Action ID
parameters.description: Specify the ID of the query action that generated the live query results.
parameters.name: actionId
parameters.tooltip: Specify the ID of the query action that generated the live query results.

operations.title: Search cases
operations.description: Search cases in Elastic Kibana server based on the input parameters that you have specified.

parameters.title: Assignees
parameters.description: Filters the returned cases by assignees. Valid values are None or unique identifiers for the user profiles.
parameters.name: assignees
parameters.tooltip: Filters the returned cases by assignees. Valid values are None or unique identifiers for the user profiles.

parameters.title: Category
parameters.description: Filters the returned cases by category.
parameters.name: category
parameters.tooltip: Filters the returned cases by category.

parameters.title: Default Search Operator
parameters.description: Specify the default operator to use for the simple query string. By default, it set as "OR"
parameters.name: defaultSearchOperator
parameters.tooltip: Specify the default operator to use for the simple query string. By default, it set as "OR"

parameters.title: From DateTime
parameters.description: Specify the from datetime to retrieve only cases that were created after a specific date. The date must be specified as a KQL data range or date match expression.
parameters.name: from_
parameters.tooltip: Specify the from datetime to retrieve only cases that were created after a specific date. The date must be specified as a KQL data range or date match expression.

parameters.title: To DateTime
parameters.description: Specify the to datetime, to only cases that were created before a specific date. The date must be specified as a KQL data range or date match expression.
parameters.name: to
parameters.tooltip: Specify the to datetime, to only cases that were created before a specific date. The date must be specified as a KQL data range or date match expression.

parameters.title: Owner
parameters.description: A filter to limit the response to a specific set of applications. If this parameter is omitted, the response contains information about all the cases that the user has access to read. You can choose from the following options: Cases, Observability, or Security Solution
parameters.name: owner
parameters.tooltip: A filter to limit the response to a specific set of applications. If this parameter is omitted, the response contains information about all the cases that the user has access to read. You can choose from the following options: Cases, Observability, or Security Solution

parameters.title: Reporters
parameters.description: Filters the returned cases by the user name of the reporter.
parameters.name: reporters
parameters.tooltip: Filters the returned cases by the user name of the reporter.

parameters.title: Search Query
parameters.description: An Elasticsearch simple query string query that filters the objects in the response.
parameters.name: search
parameters.tooltip: An Elasticsearch simple query string query that filters the objects in the response.

parameters.title: Search Fields
parameters.description: Select the fields to perform the simple_query_string parsed query against. You can choose either Title or Description
parameters.name: searchFields
parameters.tooltip: Select the fields to perform the simple_query_string parsed query against. You can choose either Title or Description

parameters.title: Severity
parameters.description: Select the severity by which to filters the returned cases. You can choose from the following options: Low, Medium, high, or Critical.
parameters.name: severity
parameters.tooltip: Select the severity by which to filters the returned cases. You can choose from the following options: Low, Medium, high, or Critical.

parameters.title: Status
parameters.description: Select the status by which to filters the returned cases by state. You can choose from the following options: Open, In Progress, or Closed
parameters.name: status
parameters.tooltip: Select the status by which to filters the returned cases by state. You can choose from the following options: Open, In Progress, or Closed

parameters.title: Tags
parameters.description: Filters the returned cases by tags.
parameters.name: tags
parameters.tooltip: Filters the returned cases by tags.

parameters.title: Sort Field
parameters.description: Select the fields by which to sort the cases retrieved. You can choose from the following options: CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, ClosedAt, Title, category, Status, or Severity. By default, this option is set to "CreatedAt".
parameters.name: sortField
parameters.tooltip: Select the fields by which to sort the cases retrieved. You can choose from the following options: CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, ClosedAt, Title, category, Status, or Severity. By default, this option is set to "CreatedAt".

parameters.title: Sort Order
parameters.description: Select the sort order for the retrieved cases. You can choose from the following options: Ascending or Descending. By default, this option is set to "Descending".
parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.tooltip: Select the sort order for the retrieved cases. You can choose from the following options: Ascending or Descending. By default, this option is set to "Descending".

parameters.title: Page Number
parameters.description: Specify the page number from which to retrieve cases when pagination is applied. This parameter is used to fetch a specific subset of results based on the page number, helping to break large datasets into manageable chunks. By default, this value is set to 1.
parameters.name: page
parameters.tooltip: Specify the page number from which to retrieve cases when pagination is applied. This parameter is used to fetch a specific subset of results based on the page number, helping to break large datasets into manageable chunks. By default, this value is set to 1.

parameters.title: Page Size
parameters.description: Specify the maximum number of results this operation should return, per page, in the response. By default, this value is set to 20 and maximum value is 100.
parameters.name: perPage
parameters.tooltip: Specify the maximum number of results this operation should return, per page, in the response. By default, this value is set to 20 and maximum value is 100.

operations.title: Get Case Information
operations.description: Retrieve a specific case information from Elastic Kibana based on the case ID you have specified.

parameters.title: Case ID
parameters.description: Specify the ID of the case based on which you want to retrieve details.
parameters.name: caseId
parameters.tooltip: Specify the ID of the case based on which you want to retrieve details.

operations.title: Add and Remove Detection Alert Tags
operations.description: Add and remove detection alert tags based on the alert IDs and tags which you have specified.

parameters.title: Alert IDs
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of alert IDs to add and remove detection alert tags in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: ids
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of alert IDs to add and remove detection alert tags in Elastic Kibana. For e.g ["id_1", "id_2", ...]

parameters.title: Tags
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of alert tags to add and remove from alerts in Elastic Kibana.
parameters.name: tags
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of alert tags to add and remove from alerts in Elastic Kibana. For e.g {"tags_to_add": ["Duplicated"], "tags_to_remove": []}

operations.title: Execute an API Request
operations.description: Sends an API request to an API endpoint based on specified HTTP method, endpoint, and other input parameters that you have specified, enabling flexible API interactions tailored to user needs.

parameters.title: HTTP Method
parameters.description:
Select an HTTP action for the request. You can select from the following options: 

GET 

PUT 

POST 

DELETE 

PATCH 

HEAD 

OPTIONS 

TRACE
parameters.name: method

parameters.title: Endpoint
parameters.description: Specify the target API URL path for the request. For example, if the website is https://example.com and URL path is https://example.com/images/pic.jpg, the endpoint would be /images/pic.jpg.
parameters.name: api_endpoint
parameters.tooltip: Specify the target API URL path for example: /api/data_views

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.description: Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.
parameters.name: params
parameters.tooltip: Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.

parameters.title: Request Payload
parameters.description: Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).
parameters.name: json_data
parameters.tooltip: Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).

operations.title: (Deprecation Warning) Generic Action
operations.description: Sends an API request to an API endpoint based on specified HTTP method, endpoint, and other input parameters that you have specified, enabling flexible API interactions tailored to user needs.

parameters.title: HTTP Method
parameters.description:
Select an HTTP action for the request. You can select from the following options: 

GET 

PUT 

POST 

DELETE 

PATCH
parameters.name: method

parameters.title: Endpoint
parameters.description: Specify the target API URL path for the request. For example, if the website is https://example.com and URL path is https://example.com/images/pic.jpg, the endpoint would be /images/pic.jpg.
parameters.name: apiendpoint
parameters.tooltip: Specify the target API URL path for example: /api/data_views

parameters.title: Request Payload
parameters.description: Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).
parameters.name: data
parameters.tooltip: Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.description: Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.
parameters.name: params
parameters.tooltip: Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.
